Name: Upvest
Long description: Upvest, founded in 2017 and based in Berlin, is a fintech startup that empowers financial institutions to integrate investment products into their applications through a modular and scalable API. The company operates in the embedded finance market, providing a comprehensive solution that includes regulatory licenses, making it easier for businesses to offer investment opportunities to their users. Upvest's business model revolves around offering its API as a service, generating revenue through subscription fees and transaction-based pricing. The company serves a diverse range of clients, including banks, fintech companies, and other financial institutions, helping them to enhance their product offerings and improve user engagement. Upvest is supported by the European Union and the European Union Regional Development Fund, and it is also involved in research and development projects aimed at improving blockchain transaction fee prediction tools. The startup's team comprises experts from renowned institutions such as Transferwise, Onfido, Klarna, Goldman Sachs, and UBS, ensuring a high level of expertise in capital markets, compliance, security, and technology.Keywords: Investment API, embedded finance, fintech, digital assets, regulatory licenses, scalable solutions, capital markets, blockchain, financial institutions, Berlin startup.
Founders: Martin Kassing;Tobias Auferoth;Juha Ristolainen;Ivan Morozov;Jesper Noehr;Til Rochow

Name: Ravio
Long description: Ravio.com is a cutting-edge startup that operates in the tech industry, providing real-time salary and benefits benchmarks for tech companies across Europe. The company's primary clients are tech companies looking to develop a competitive and fair compensation strategy. Ravio.com offers a comprehensive set of tools that allow businesses to build, develop, and manage their compensation strategies effectively. The company's business model revolves around providing access to live compensation data from leading tech companies. This data is a crucial tool for businesses to build a compensation strategy that is not only fair but also competitive. This data-driven approach eliminates guesswork and outdated spreadsheets, allowing businesses to make informed decisions about their compensation strategies. Ravio.com also offers insights into market trends, such as pay increases, hiring and attrition rates, and popular benefits packages. This information is crucial for businesses to stay competitive in the rapidly evolving tech industry. The company makes money by providing these services to tech companies. By partnering with Ravio.com, businesses can access reliable benchmarking data for the positions they are hiring for, saving them time and resources. In summary, Ravio.com is a valuable tool for tech companies looking to develop a competitive and fair compensation strategy. The company's data-driven approach allows businesses to make informed decisions about their compensation strategies, helping them to attract and retain top talent in the competitive tech industry. Keywords: Compensation Strategy, Tech Industry, Real-Time Salary Benchmarks, Benefits Benchmarks, Data-Driven Decisions, Market Trends, Competitive Pay Practices, Equity Pay, Variable Pay, Hiring and Retention.
Founders: Raymond Siems;Merten Wulfert;Roy Blanga;Evan Martin;Wilson Griffiths

Name: NG.CASH
Long description: NG.Cash is a digital banking platform that is revolutionizing the financial landscape for the younger generation. This startup operates in the fintech market, providing an easy-to-use and practical banking solution. The platform is designed to be user-friendly, making it an excellent tool for young people to learn money management. The business model of NG.Cash is centered around providing digital banking services. It offers a range of financial solutions that are easily accessible through their mobile application. The platform's primary target audience is the younger generation, who are increasingly becoming more financially aware and are seeking convenient and efficient ways to manage their money. NG.Cash makes money by offering premium financial services. While the basic functionalities are available to all users, additional features and services may come at a cost. This freemium model allows the company to cater to a broad range of customers, from those seeking basic banking services to those who require more advanced financial solutions.The company also places a strong emphasis on customer service, with a dedicated support team available from Monday to Friday, 9 am to 6 pm. This commitment to customer service enhances the overall user experience, making NG.Cash a reliable and trustworthy digital banking solution.In summary, NG.Cash is a dynamic startup that is transforming the way young people interact with financial services. Its user-friendly platform, coupled with a range of financial solutions, makes it a standout player in the fintech market.Keywords: Digital Banking, Fintech, Young Generation, Money Management, User-Friendly, Mobile Application, Premium Services, Freemium Model, Customer Service, Financial Solutions.
Founders: Petrus Ballhausen Arruda;Luis Felipe Carvalho;Mario Augusto Sá;Victor Eagle Trindade;Gabriel Fernandes;Antônio Nakad;Victor Trindade

Name: Skrill
Long description: Skrill is a digital wallet provider that facilitates online payments and money transfers for both individual and business clients. Established in the United Kingdom in 2001 as Moneybookers by Daniel Klein and Benjamin Kullmann, the company was among the first to receive an electronic money license from the Financial Services Authority. Klein, a serial tech entrepreneur, later co-founded the mobile point-of-sale company SumUp. Kullmann is now involved in global philanthropic work through the Kullmann Foundation. The company experienced rapid growth, reaching nearly two million users within its first 1.5 years. In 2007, Investcorp acquired the company, which was later rebranded to Skrill starting in 2011. A series of acquisitions followed, including the purchase of paysafecard in 2013 and Ukash in 2015. In 2015, Skrill itself was acquired for €1.1 billion by Optimal Payments, the parent company of competitor Neteller, which subsequently rebranded as Paysafe. Today, Skrill operates as one of Paysafe's digital wallet brands. The platform allows users to send and receive money in over 40 currencies across more than 100 countries. Its core service is a digital wallet that securely stores users' payment details, enabling them to pay online using just an email address and password. For businesses, Skrill offers merchant accounts that support over 100 local payment methods, credit and debit cards, and bank transfers. The business model relies on fees charged for transactions such as withdrawals, currency conversions, and certain types of money transfers. The platform also generates revenue from its VIP program and services tailored to the online gaming and gambling industry. Since 2018, Skrill has expanded its services to include cryptocurrency trading, allowing users to buy and sell various digital currencies directly from their wallets. Keywords: digital wallet, online payments, money transfer, e-commerce, payment processing, cross-border payments, cryptocurrency trading, prepaid card, payment gateway, fintech

Name: Blockbrain
Long description: The Block Brain is a comprehensive cryptocurrency risk management platform. It provides a suite of tools designed to help individual and institutional investors navigate the volatile and complex world of digital assets. The platform operates in the rapidly growing cryptocurrency market, serving a broad range of clients from individual investors to large financial institutions.The Block Brain's business model revolves around providing real-time risk assessment and portfolio optimization services. It offers a 360-degree threat report that includes smart contract audits and the ability to spot dangerous approvals across all crypto wallets. The platform also provides real-time alerts about activity that could affect a client's portfolio, scanning millions of blockchain transactions 24/7. In addition, the platform offers a crypto exchange monitor that notifies users about unusual activities on major crypto exchanges. It also allows users to track any Ethereum wallet, hot or cold, without requiring a wallet connect. The platform's AI risk advisor provides personalized guidance for managing and adjusting a DeFi portfolio based on the user's risk profile and investment goals.The Block Brain also offers a feature to track the performance of crypto and NFTs across every wallet a user owns. It provides personalized news and updates about web3 projects in a user's watchlist, filtering information from Twitter, Discord, and news articles into one place. The team behind The Block Brain has over 60 years of combined experience in scaling data-driven tech companies. They leverage their expertise in AI, data, software engineering, and web3 to accelerate the adoption of blockchain technology.Keywords: Cryptocurrency, Risk Management, Portfolio Optimization, Real-Time Alerts, Blockchain Transactions, Crypto Exchange Monitor, Ethereum Wallet Tracking, AI Risk Advisor, Performance Tracking, Personalized News.

Name: EMIL
Long description: EMIL is a German company that provides a no-code software suite for the insurance industry. The "EMIL Insurance Suite" is a platform that enables insurance companies to manage existing portfolios more cost-effectively and to launch new and innovative insurance products quickly. The platform is designed to be operated and managed by the insurance companies themselves, without the need for IT specialists or coding skills.The company was founded in 2016 by Bastian Knutzen and Chris Maslowski, who aimed to revolutionize the insurance sector with modern technology. EMIL's business model is likely based on a B2B software-as-a-service (SaaS) model, where they charge insurance companies a license fee to use their platform. Their target clients are insurance companies of all sizes that are looking to modernize their operations and product offerings.By providing a no-code platform, EMIL addresses a key challenge in the insurance industry: the long development cycles and high costs associated with launching new products and updating legacy IT systems. The platform's ability to be managed by non-technical staff can also lead to significant cost savings for their clients.Keywords: insurance technology, no-code platform, portfolio management, product launch, SaaS, B2B, insurtech, policy administration, claims management, underwriting

Name: Raisin
Long description: Raisin is a financial technology company that operates an online marketplace for savings and investment products. The company provides a platform that connects retail customers with a wide range of banks across Europe and the United States, offering them the opportunity to access competitive interest rates on savings products, such as term deposits and high-yield savings accounts, from a single online account.The business model is centered on a B2C approach, targeting individual savers who are looking for better returns on their cash deposits than what is typically available from their local banks. Raisin also has a B2B offering, providing its technology and marketplace to banks and other financial institutions that want to offer their own customers a broader range of savings options.The company earns revenue through commissions paid by the partner banks for the deposits they gather through the platform. This model allows Raisin to offer its service for free to retail customers. The market it operates in is the retail banking and savings sector, with a focus on cross-border deposit gathering.Keywords: savings marketplace, investment platform, fintech, deposit brokerage, retail banking, interest rates, term deposits, financial technology, B2B, B2C

Name: Langfuse
Long description: Langfuse is a startup that operates in the tech industry, providing open-source observability and analytics for Language Learning Machine (LLM) applications. The company's primary focus is on helping clients trace and debug their increasingly complex LLM applications. This is achieved by providing detailed production traces and a granular view on quality, cost, and latency of these applications.Langfuse's services are designed for agents and LLM chains. The company allows for the tracing of unlimited nested actions, providing a detailed view of the entire request. This includes exact cost calculation, tokenizing prompts and completions of popular models to measure the cost of each step of the LLM chain precisely.In addition to LLM actions, Langfuse also tracks non-LLM actions such as database queries and API calls. This feature provides optimal visibility into issues, enhancing the overall application performance. The company's services are open and integrated, working with all models and configurations, and offering native integrations with popular frameworks and libraries.Langfuse's business model is based on data ingestion. The company provides prebuilt analytics to help teams focus on the most important metrics. These analytics include tracking token usage and cost, adding scores to each trace, and monitoring and improving latency. All analytics are connected to traces, making it easy to find the root cause of any issue.The company also offers a public API, allowing clients to build their own custom features and dashboards on top of Langfuse. It also provides typed Software Development Kits (SDKs) for Python and JS/TS, giving clients full control over what is sent to Langfuse.Keywords: Open-source observability, Analytics, Language Learning Machine (LLM) applications, Tracing, Debugging, Cost calculation, Non-LLM actions tracking, Prebuilt analytics, Public API, Software Development Kits (SDKs).

Name: Zlick.it
Long description: Zlick is a London-based fintech company that provides a payment and subscription management platform for digital content creators and publishers. The company's primary product is a paywall solution that enables businesses to monetize their online content through subscriptions and single payments. Zlick's platform is designed for easy integration, with a WordPress plugin available, and boasts a fast setup time.The company serves a range of clients, including digital publishers, classifieds websites, and video-on-demand providers. Zlick's business model is based on helping these clients increase their digital revenue by converting casual visitors into paying subscribers. In addition to its paywall solution, Zlick offers features to enhance customer engagement and retention, such as a newsletter tool for upselling and community building. The company also provides bespoke website design services for content creators, further expanding its value proposition.Keywords: Fintech, Paywall, Subscription Management, Content Monetization, Digital Payments, Publisher Services, Creator Economy, SaaS, E-commerce, London

Name: Kuunda
Long description: Kuunda is a FinTech incubator and advisory company focused on developing sustainable financial products with a positive social impact. The company primarily serves Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) and Mobile Money agents in developing economies. Kuunda operates in the financial technology sector, providing data and agent liquidity tools to enhance financial inclusion and operational efficiency. The business model revolves around creating scalable ecosystems in partnership with other organizations, leveraging data analytics to optimize financial services. Kuunda generates revenue through advisory services, product development, and data solutions tailored to the needs of its clients. The company aims to improve Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) for agents by providing daily recommendations and predictive tools to manage eFloat investments effectively.Keywords: FinTech, incubator, advisory, financial inclusion, MNOs, Mobile Money, data analytics, liquidity tools, developing economies, scalable ecosystems.
Founders: Samuel Brawerman;Morné Van Der Westhuizen;Morné van der Westhuizen

Name: SumUp
Long description: SumUp is a financial technology startup that provides easy, secure, and affordable card payment solutions for small businesses. The company offers a range of card readers that allow businesses to accept credit and debit card payments seamlessly. SumUp operates in multiple markets, including the United States, the United Kingdom, Germany, France, and many other countries across Europe and South America.SumUp primarily serves small to medium-sized businesses (SMBs) that need a reliable and cost-effective way to process card payments. These clients range from local shops and cafes to service providers and freelancers. The company’s card readers are designed to be portable and user-friendly, making them ideal for businesses that operate in various environments, whether at a fixed location or on the go.The business model of SumUp revolves around selling card readers and charging a transaction fee for each payment processed. The company offers transparent pricing with no monthly costs or contracts, charging a flat rate of 2.75% per in-person transaction. This straightforward pricing structure makes it easier for small businesses to manage their expenses without worrying about hidden fees or long-term commitments.SumUp makes money through the sale of its card readers and the transaction fees collected from each payment processed. By providing a combination of hardware and payment processing services, SumUp creates a steady revenue stream while helping businesses grow by enabling them to accept card payments easily.In summary, SumUp is a fintech company that empowers small businesses to accept card payments with ease and security. It operates in a global market, serving SMBs with portable and affordable card readers, and generates revenue through hardware sales and transaction fees.Keywords: fintech, card readers, small businesses, payment solutions, transaction fees, portable, secure, global market, transparent pricing, SMBs.
Founders: Jan Deepen;Stefan Jeschonnek;Daniel Klein;Petter Made;Marc-Alexander Christ

Name: Ultramarin
Long description: Based in Berlin, Germany, Ultramarin GmbH is a financial technology firm established in 2017 that develops artificial intelligence-driven investment solutions for professional investors. The company was founded by Daniel Willmann, Mathias Eitz, and Ronald Richter, who brought together their interdisciplinary expertise in finance, computer science, and machine learning. Daniel Willmann, the CEO, has a background in business administration and professional services firms in Munich, Frankfurt, and London. CTO Mathias Eitz holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science and has prior experience founding software development and AI companies. Ronald Richter, a Principal Machine Learning Engineer, has over 25 years of experience in professional software development, specializing in complex systems analysis.Ultramarin operates in the quantitative asset management sector, serving clients such as banks, family offices, and financial intermediaries. The firm’s business model is centered on providing AI-based predictive models for global equity markets to enhance investment decisions. Revenue is generated through several offerings: investment funds providing direct access to AI-based equity strategies, customized solutions via individual mandates, and participation in the performance of AI-optimized equity indices through ETFs. Additionally, the company provides API-based access to its AI-powered equity research platform, Ultrascope, which covers over 2,000 companies globally.The core of Ultramarin's service is its proprietary AI platform, which models complex market dynamics and integrates various data sets, including those from fundamental analysis and behavioral finance. This platform is designed to identify non-linear relationships that traditional econometric models might miss, aiming for improved prediction quality. A notable feature is the "Fast-and-Slow" approach to tactical equity allocation, inspired by Daniel Kahneman's work, which models both intuitive and rational market behaviors to act as an early warning system. The solutions are highly customizable, allowing clients to tailor strategies based on specific investment universes, risk management parameters, and sustainability (ESG) criteria. A significant milestone was the June 2024 launch of the first AI-driven active equity ETF in Europe, the AI-Enhanced Eurozone Equities UCITS ETF, in partnership with Baader Bank AG and supported by the Goldman Sachs ETF Accelerator.Keywords: quantitative asset management, artificial intelligence, financial modeling, investment strategies, fintech, machine learning, equity research, asset management, predictive analytics, algorithmic trading

Name: Selina Finance
Long description: Selina Finance is a UK-based fintech startup that offers flexible lending solutions to homeowners. The company operates in the financial services market, specifically in the home equity lending sector. It provides two main products: a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) and a Homeowner Loan. The HELOC is a revolving credit facility that allows homeowners to borrow against the equity in their property. This product is unique in the UK market and offers a high degree of flexibility. Borrowers only pay interest on the funds they draw down, not on the full credit limit, and they can adjust their borrowing up or down as needed over the first five years. The Homeowner Loan, on the other hand, provides borrowers with a fixed amount of funds on day one, which is secured against their property. This product is more suitable for those who need a large sum of money all at once. Selina Finance's business model is based on earning interest from the loans it provides. The company offers loans ranging from Ł10k to Ł250k, with interest rates starting at 7.70% per annum. The company's target clients are homeowners who have built up equity in their property and are looking to finance large purchases or projects. By leveraging the equity in their homes, these homeowners can access high loan amounts at relatively low interest rates. Keywords: Fintech, Home Equity Line of Credit, Homeowner Loan, Home Equity, Lending, Financial Services, UK Market, Interest Rates, Large Purchases, Homeowners.

Name: The Landbanking Group
Long description: The Landbanking Group, a Munich-based environmental growth company, has developed a platform to transform nature-positive outcomes into tradable assets. The company's core offering is a B2B SaaS platform called Landler, which allows businesses to monitor, report, and verify the impact of their investments in nature. This enables them to assess climate risk in their supply chains and invest in regenerative practices.The Landbanking Group's business model is centered on creating a new asset class focused on climate regeneration through land investments. By providing a market infrastructure for these assets, the company aims to make the preservation and restoration of nature economically viable. The company serves clients across various industries, including organic food and cocoa, helping them to monitor the effectiveness of regenerative agriculture in their supply chains.Keywords: nature-based assets, climate regeneration, land valuation, B2B, SaaS, environmental markets, regenerative agriculture, supply chain monitoring, impact investing, ecosystem services
Founders: Prof. Martin R. Stuchtey;Dr. Sonja Stuchtey;Tobias Bandel

Name: Fluz
Long description: Fluz is a financial technology company that operates a collaborative earning app designed to maximize users' money through cashback on everyday purchases. The app serves a diverse clientele, including individual consumers looking to save on daily expenses and small business owners aiming to streamline their financial operations. Fluz operates in the fintech market, leveraging partnerships with FDIC-member banks to provide banking services. The business model revolves around earning revenue through transaction fees and partnerships with merchants who offer digital e-gift card codes. Users can make and receive payments, earn cashback, and even form LLCs through the platform. The app also encourages users to grow their network, thereby increasing their earning potential. Keywords: fintech, cashback, collaborative earning, digital payments, e-gift cards, small business, LLC formation, partner banks, transaction fees, network growth.

Name: Seedflex
Long description: Seedflex is a financial technology company that provides innovative financing solutions tailored for online merchants. Operating in the fintech sector, Seedflex offers a unique 'Pay As You Sell' model, allowing merchants to repay loans automatically as a share of their online sales. This approach ensures that businesses can maintain control over their capital and growth without the burden of fixed monthly payments. Seedflex integrates with multiple e-commerce platforms to meet the full credit needs of merchants, offering a seamless and frictionless experience. The company primarily serves small to medium-sized online businesses looking for flexible and scalable financing options. Seedflex's business model revolves around earning revenue through interest and fees on the loans provided to merchants. By leveraging dynamic and granular transaction data, Seedflex enhances its underwriting process, ensuring robust and reliable credit assessments. The company also partners with other fintech firms to expand its reach and improve its service offerings. Keywords: fintech, online merchants, flexible financing, Pay As You Sell, e-commerce integration, dynamic underwriting, capital management, scalable loans, transaction data, B2C merchants.

Name: dentolo
Long description: dentolo is a dental insurance provider that offers various plans to cover the costs of dental treatments. The company provides different tariffs, such as "Zahnschutz Komfort" and "Premium", with coverage ranging from 75% to 100% of the dental bill. This allows customers to choose a plan that best suits their needs and budget.The business model is centered around providing a digital-first experience, making it easy for customers to manage their insurance online. This includes services like getting a quote, signing up for a plan, and accessing customer support through email and online contact forms. The company targets individuals who are looking for comprehensive dental coverage and prefer the convenience of a digital platform.dentolo makes money by selling these dental insurance plans. The company emphasizes preventive care and aims to make dental health more accessible and affordable for its customers. By offering a range of plans with different levels of coverage, dentolo can cater to a wide market of individuals seeking to supplement their statutory health insurance.Keywords: dental insurance, insurtech, dental care, health insurance, digital health, preventive care, dental plans, online insurance, cost coverage, dental treatments

Name: Tillhub
Long description: Tillhub, operating as Tillhub by Unzer, offers a cloud-based, all-in-one point-of-sale (POS) system designed for a diverse client base that includes retailers, service providers, and hospitality businesses. Founded in Berlin in 2015 by Tim Seithe, Oliver Mickler, Frank Seithe, and others, the company aimed to help businesses digitize their operations. Co-founder Oliver Mickler brought experience from roles at Deutsche Bank, Credit Suisse, and as the founder of myDriver.com, while Tim Seithe holds a Doctor of Medicine degree and has a background as an angel investor. The company's core offering is an iPad-based POS system that combines traditional cashiering with broader business management functionalities. The platform provides tools for inventory management, sales analytics, customer management, employee administration, and DATEV export for accounting. Tillhub serves a range of business sizes, from small and medium-sized companies to large enterprises and franchise chains, with specific product variations for retail, gastronomy, and service industries. For example, the gastronomy version includes features like table plans and happy hour functions, while the retail system focuses on inventory control and customer retention tools. A mobile, Android-based device called POS Go combines the POS system, a card terminal, a barcode scanner, and a receipt printer into a single unit. The business generates revenue through monthly subscriptions, with pricing dependent on the selected hardware and software packages. Tillhub's journey includes securing approximately $4.89 million in funding over two rounds, with notable investors like Elavon and Heidelpay (now Unzer). A significant milestone occurred in April 2022 when the payment service provider Unzer, which had already invested in 2020, acquired the remaining shares of Tillhub for an undisclosed amount, although reports suggest a valuation of around 30 million euros. This acquisition was a strategic move for Unzer to create a unified commerce solution, integrating Tillhub's cloud-based POS software with Unzer's own payment processing capabilities for both e-commerce and brick-and-mortar retail. Following the acquisition, Tillhub became an operating subsidiary of Unzer, with its co-founders taking on senior roles within the parent company to advance the unified commerce strategy. Keywords: point-of-sale, POS system, cloud-based POS, iPad cash register, unified commerce, retail technology, gastronomy POS, service industry POS, inventory management, payment processing, customer relationship management, sales analytics, digital bookkeeping, Unzer, Tim Seithe, Oliver Mickler, Berlin startup, fintech, mobile payments, DATEV export, franchise POS

Name: Fulfin
Long description: Fulfin, operating under isarlend GmbH, is a Munich-based financial technology firm established in 2018 by co-founders Nathan Evans and Dr. Alfred “Fredi” Gruber. Dr. Gruber brings extensive experience from the finance industry, having worked for 14 years in investment banking and holding a Ph.D. in finance, while Evans has a background in the alternative lending sector. The company was founded to address a perceived gap in the market: the difficulty for rapidly growing e-commerce businesses to secure adequate financing from traditional banks.The company's primary business is providing flexible working capital and goods financing to small and midsize online retailers, particularly those operating on platforms like Amazon, eBay, or Shopify. Fulfin's business model is based on generating interest margins by refinancing its loan portfolio at a lower cost than the interest rates it offers to clients. Its core service involves a fully digitized loan application and assessment process, which allows for rapid credit decisions and disbursement, sometimes within 48 hours. The platform utilizes a proprietary AI-powered risk-scoring algorithm, Fulfin-Tech™, which analyzes a combination of bank transactions, e-commerce sales data, logistics, and inventory information to evaluate a company's creditworthiness. This data-centric approach enables Fulfin to offer loans using the client's inventory as collateral, a model less common in traditional lending.Fulfin primarily serves businesses based in Germany across various sectors including e-commerce, wholesale, retail, and skilled trades. The firm has established strategic partnerships to enhance its offerings, including a banking-as-a-service and financing cooperation with Varengold Bank since May 2022. The company has secured several rounds of funding, including a seven-figure seed round in 2019 and a Series A round of €4.35 million in August 2022, with investors such as Hevella Capital, Lakeside, and Stableton Financial. Beyond lending, Fulfin is developing analysis and benchmark products to provide its customers with greater financial insights.Keywords: e-commerce financing, working capital loans, online retailer funding, merchandise financing, digital lending, fintech, SME lending, alternative financing, inventory-based loans, supply chain finance, revenue-based financing, business loans Germany, Fulfin-Tech, Alfred Gruber, Nathan Evans, Varengold Bank, Hevella Capital, Lakeside, small business finance, growth capital, digital credit platform

Name: CarbonPool
Long description: CarbonPool, headquartered in Switzerland, is a pioneering startup in the niche market of carbon credit insurance. The company offers a unique insurance product that pays claims in carbon credits instead of cash. This innovative approach is designed to serve developers and buyers of carbon credits, providing them with a safety net against potential risks associated with their investments in carbon offset projects.CarbonPool operates in the environmental finance sector, specifically targeting the carbon credit market. This market involves the trading of carbon credits, which are permits allowing the holder to emit a certain amount of carbon dioxide or other greenhouse gases. Companies and individuals purchase these credits to offset their carbon footprint, contributing to global efforts to combat climate change.The business model of CarbonPool revolves around providing insurance policies that mitigate the risks for both developers and buyers of carbon credits. Developers, who create carbon offset projects, and buyers, who invest in these credits, face uncertainties such as project underperformance or regulatory changes. CarbonPool's insurance products are designed to cover these risks, ensuring that stakeholders can confidently invest in carbon credits.CarbonPool generates revenue by charging premiums for its insurance policies. These premiums are paid by the clients—developers and buyers—who seek to protect their investments in carbon credits. The company leverages the expertise of its seasoned team, which includes professionals with extensive backgrounds in insurance, financial solutions, and climate science, to accurately assess risks and price their insurance products competitively.Key personnel at CarbonPool include Beat Krauer, the Chief Underwriting Officer, who has over 20 years of experience in the reinsurance market, and Coenraad Vrolijk, the CEO and co-founder, who has a rich background in financial services and insurance. Their combined expertise ensures that CarbonPool is well-positioned to navigate the complexities of the carbon credit market and provide reliable insurance solutions.Keywords: carbon credit insurance, environmental finance, carbon offset, risk mitigation, insurance premiums, climate science, financial solutions, reinsurance, carbon market, investment protection.

Name: Myos
Long description: Myos is a pioneering financial technology company that provides working capital solutions specifically tailored for ecommerce businesses. Operating in the fintech sector, Myos leverages an AI-based risk scoring model to offer product-backed financing, enabling businesses to unlock the value of their inventory and assets. This innovative approach allows ecommerce companies to manage short-term cash flow fluctuations, cover unexpected expenses, and invest in growth initiatives without excessive financial strain. Myos serves a diverse range of clients, from burgeoning startups to well-established enterprises, providing flexible financing options that adjust to the unique cash flow needs of each business. The company's business model revolves around earning interest on the capital provided, with fixed repayment periods and interest rates ensuring transparency and predictability for clients. By separating business finances from personal finances, Myos helps maintain compliance and transparency, fostering long-term success for its clients. With over 40 years of combined experience in finance, technology, and company building, the Myos management team is committed to breaking new ground in the global financial landscape. Keywords: AI-driven, working capital, ecommerce, product-backed financing, fintech, risk scoring, inventory value, cash flow management, growth investment, financial transparency.

Name: A352
Long description: A352.io is a fintech startup that offers an integrated financial management solution called the Financial Navigator. This platform is designed to streamline and optimize cash management and payment processes for businesses. The Financial Navigator connects to 4900 banks through various secure methods such as Host 2 Host, EBICS, or PSD2, allowing businesses to manage their finances centrally and independently of their banks.The primary clients of A352.io are businesses looking to enhance their financial operations. These clients range from small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to larger corporations that need efficient tools to manage their cash flow, payments, and financial risks. The market A352.io operates in is the financial technology (fintech) sector, which focuses on improving and automating the delivery and use of financial services.A352.io's business model revolves around providing a subscription-based service where businesses pay for access to the Financial Navigator. This model ensures a steady revenue stream while offering clients continuous updates and support. The company makes money by charging fees for the use of its platform, which includes features like multibanking, payment factory, cash flow forecasting, business planning, and analytics and reporting.Key benefits of the Financial Navigator include real-time visibility of liquidity positions, centralized payment management, fraud prevention through standardized processes and permission management, and enhanced decision-making with accurate cash flow forecasts and automated analytics. By using this platform, businesses can identify new growth opportunities, reduce financial risks and costs, and simplify their overall financial management.Keywords: fintech, cash management, payment processes, multibanking, financial risk, cost reduction, liquidity, business planning, analytics, fraud prevention.

Name: Käuferportal
Long description: Kaeuferportal.de operates as a digital marketplace that connects consumers with verified local service providers for various home improvement projects. The platform serves individual homeowners and small businesses looking to undertake renovations, installations, or repairs. Operating primarily in the German market, Kaeuferportal.de leverages a network of vetted professionals to ensure high-quality service delivery. The business model revolves around lead generation, where service providers pay for access to potential clients who have expressed interest in their services. Revenue is generated through these lead fees and potentially through premium listings or advertising. The platform simplifies the process of finding reliable contractors, thereby saving time and reducing the risk of subpar work for its users. Keywords: home improvement, digital marketplace, lead generation, verified professionals, renovations, installations, repairs, German market, service providers, quality assurance.

Name: Degura
Long description: Degura is a FinTech company specializing in digital company pension solutions, primarily serving HR professionals and businesses looking to simplify their pension administration processes. Operating in the financial technology sector, Degura offers a cloud-based platform known as the Degura Pension Cloud, which integrates seamlessly with existing HR systems through API connections. The platform is designed to be user-friendly and modern, significantly easing the administrative burden associated with managing company pension plans (bAV). Degura's business model revolves around providing subscription-based access to its platform, generating revenue through service fees and premium features. The company has recently secured EUR 2.2M in seed funding to enhance its offerings and expand its market reach. Name: FANtium
Long description: FANtium is a startup dedicated to revolutionizing the sports industry by giving fans a real stake in their favorite sports. The company operates in the sports and entertainment market, focusing on creating a deeper connection between fans and athletes. FANtium's unique business model allows fans to gain ownership in sports, fostering a sense of shared success and community.The platform serves sports enthusiasts who are passionate about supporting their favorite athletes and teams. By leveraging blockchain technology, FANtium enables fans to invest in athletes, providing them with resources, funding, and opportunities that go beyond traditional sponsorships and endorsements. This model not only benefits the athletes by giving them access to necessary resources but also allows fans to feel more involved and invested in the sports they love.FANtium makes money by facilitating these investments and partnerships between fans and athletes. Name: Riskine
Long description: Based in Vienna, Austria, Riskine operates as a B2B software provider for the financial services industry, specializing in digital advisory and sales solutions. The company was founded in 2016 by Dr. Ralf Widtmann, who now serves as CEO. His background as a strategy consultant for various insurance companies, including a role as a board assistant at Allianz and a senior project manager at Roland Berger, provided the foundational experience for Riskine's focus. In May 2024, the firm was acquired by the Swiss software company BSI Business Systems Integration, though it continues to operate as its own brand.  Riskine's business model is centered on providing white-label, API-based software to its client base of over 50 insurance companies, banks, and financial distributors across 12 markets. The company generates revenue by offering its modular platform as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution, which can be implemented within 3-5 months. This approach allows clients to customize solutions by combining different modules to fit their specific needs, from lead generation to closing and ongoing customer support.  The core of Riskine's offering is its AI and graph-based technology designed to enhance customer-centricity in financial advice. The platform features over 40 API-based modules, including tools for risk analysis, product recommendations, and financial planning. Its intelligent financial planning algorithm, "ALIS," integrates these modules into a holistic lifecycle model. For end-users, this translates into a transparent process that visualizes objective risks and aligns them with subjective preferences and financial goals. For the business clients, this means a more efficient and compliant advisory process, with capabilities for automated policy renewals, real-time claims tracking, and detailed analytics to monitor performance and sales trends.  Keywords: InsurTech, FinTech, digital advisory, B2B software, financial planning algorithm, white-label software, SaaS, API-based platform, risk analysis, lead generation, customer-centric finance, graph technology, AI in finance, insurance technology, banking software, compliance management, sales automation, financial services software, wealth management technology, client lifecycle management

Name: Finoa

Finoa serves institutional clients, including financial institutions, corporations, and high-net-worth individuals, who seek secure and compliant ways to invest in and manage crypto assets. Name: Bullfinch Capital
Long description: Bullfinch Capital is a startup that operates in the health and fitness industry, specifically catering to the needs of bodybuilders in the USA. The company's primary offering is a product known as the Test Deca Anavar cycle, a popular steroid cycle known for its ability to promote significant muscle growth, strength gains, and fat loss. The Test Deca Anavar cycle combines three powerful steroids - Testosterone, Deca, and Anavar. Testosterone and Deca are known for their ability to promote muscle growth, while Anavar aids in burning fat and maintaining lean muscle mass. This combination can help bodybuilders achieve impressive gains in both size and strength. In addition to muscle growth and strength gains, the Test Deca Anavar cycle also improves endurance and recovery. It increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen delivery to the muscles and reducing fatigue. Anavar further aids in improving recovery time between workouts, allowing bodybuilders to train more frequently and intensely.However, the company emphasizes the responsible use of these steroids due to potential risks and side effects, such as liver damage, negative effects on cholesterol levels, acne, hair loss, and changes in mood and libido. They recommend using these steroids under the guidance of a licensed medical professional to minimize these risks.The business model of Bullfinch Capital is based on the sale of these steroid cycles. The company makes money by selling the Test Deca Anavar cycle to bodybuilders looking to enhance their performance and physique.Keywords: Health and Fitness, Bodybuilding, Steroid Cycle, Test Deca Anavar, Muscle Growth, Strength Gains, Fat Loss, Endurance, Recovery, Responsible Use.

Name: CapInside
Long description: CapInside GmbH, founded in Hamburg, Germany, operates as a specialized online platform for the investment market. Initially established in 2018, the firm has positioned itself as a digital, artificial intelligence-based B2B community connecting financial intermediaries, like IFAs, with asset managers and fund initiators. The company was co-founded by Philipp Schröder, who previously worked on the digitization and demonopolization of the energy industry. This background influenced CapInside's mission to bring greater transparency to the traditionally opaque investment industry. In July 2020, CapInside was acquired by The Universal-Investment Group, a major European fund service platform. This acquisition was a strategic move for Universal-Investment to enhance its digital distribution channels and build a comprehensive platform for all asset classes. Following the takeover, CapInside continues to operate as an open platform for all asset managers, benefiting from the resources and international experience of its parent company to fuel further growth. By late 2021, its registered user base had grown by over 25% to more than 26,000. The company's core service is a free-to-use platform for registered investors, offering comparison tools for over 40,000 products, including ETFs, and funds across various asset classes like equity, bonds, real estate, and crypto. The business model focuses on fund marketing and sales initiation, generating revenue by providing asset managers with services for fund distribution and marketing. This includes organizing events such as investment congresses and roadshows to connect fund initiators directly with professional investors. The platform utilizes AI-driven matching and provides high-quality content and investment analyses to facilitate these connections, effectively functioning as a digital intermediary that enhances brand awareness and distribution for its partners. Keywords: fund comparison, investment platform, asset management, financial intermediaries, B2B fintech, fund distribution, digital wealth management, portfolio analysis, fund marketing, investment community, investor relations, AI in finance, financial advisor tools, German fintech, ETF analysis, fund data, investment research, alternative investments, wealthtech, capital markets technology

Name: Clark
Long description: Clark.de is a digital insurance broker that operates in the German market. The company uses advanced technology to provide personalized insurance advice to its customers. Clark.de's primary clientele includes individuals seeking insurance coverage, whether it be health, life, or property insurance. Clark.de's business model is based on brokering insurance policies. The company makes money by earning a commission from insurance providers for every policy sold through its platform. Name: Afilio
Long description: Based in Berlin, Afilio operates as a digital platform focused on personal and family-oriented provision planning, targeting the German market. The company was founded in late 2017 by Till Oltmanns, Philip Harms, and Richard Musiol. The founders, who were in their early twenties and fresh out of university, were motivated by personal family experiences with the complexities of elder care and end-of-life bureaucracy. They identified a need to simplify the often convoluted processes associated with legal and financial planning for major life events. Afilio provides a centralized online location where individuals and families can manage legal, financial, and retirement planning. The platform enables users to generate legally sound documents such as living wills, powers of attorney, and testaments without the direct need for expensive legal counsel. It also assists with pension provisions and helps users find suitable insurance policies. The service guides users by assessing their individual needs and providing personalized, commission-free recommendations, which can be more cost-effective than traditional providers. Afilio's business model is subscription-based, offering a membership for an annual fee that provides access to its suite of document creation tools and planning services. The company's offerings are designed to serve as a digital companion through various life stages, from entering the workforce to family planning and retirement. Afilio aims to be the primary point of contact for families when dealing with emergencies or significant life events like illness or death. Since its launch, the company has secured significant funding to support its growth. Notable funding rounds include a seed round in 2019 for approximately $5 million and a Series A round in 2021 for $13 million, led by CommerzVentures with participation from investors like Cherry Ventures, Speedinvest, and Cavalry Ventures. Keywords: insurtech, legal tech, digital Vorsorge, retirement planning, estate planning, living will, power of attorney, testament generation, family protection, financial planning, pension provision, digital legal documents, end-of-life planning, elder care tech, German market, subscription model, family finance, personal provision, holistic planning, multi-generational planning, insurance management, digital inheritance, family safety net, legal document platform

Name: Exporo
Long description: Exporo is a German-based crowdinvesting platform that connects private investors with real estate developers through technology. Founded in 2014, Exporo operates in the real estate investment market, primarily serving individual investors looking for opportunities in professional and attractive real estate projects. The platform allows users to invest in real estate with relatively small amounts of capital, democratizing access to an asset class traditionally reserved for institutional investors. Exporo's business model involves sourcing real estate projects, conducting due diligence, and offering these projects to its user base for investment. Name: Oradian
Long description: Oradian, founded in 2012 by Antonio Separovic, Andrew Mainhart, Julian Oehrlein, and Josip Muzic, provides a cloud-native core banking platform tailored for financial institutions in emerging markets. The founders, drawing from their experience in banking, technology, and microfinance, identified a critical need for scalable and accessible core banking systems in underserved regions. Their collective background in these specific fields directly shaped the company's mission to drive financial inclusion. The company's headquarters is located in Zagreb, Croatia. The firm's primary offering is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform that equips microfinance institutions (MFIs), credit unions, and rural banks with the essential tools for digitalization. This platform enables clients to manage core banking operations, including client data management, loan and deposit tracking, and financial reporting, through a cloud-based system. By moving these functions to the cloud, Oradian's system allows financial institutions to scale their operations, improve efficiency, and expand their services to a broader client base, particularly in rural and remote areas. The business model is based on subscriptions to its platform, providing a predictable revenue stream. Oradian's platform is designed for accessibility and ease of use, functioning effectively even in environments with limited internet connectivity. It offers features such as real-time data access, robust security protocols, and compliance functionalities that help institutions meet regulatory requirements. A key benefit is the platform's ability to provide a comprehensive audit trail and enhance transparency, which are crucial for building trust with both customers and regulators. This focus on operational resilience and data integrity helps financial institutions to grow sustainably and serve their communities more effectively. Keywords: core banking, financial inclusion, microfinance, SaaS, fintech, emerging markets, cloud banking, financial technology, MFIs, credit unions

Name: Penta
Long description: Penta, a digital banking platform, was established in 2017 to address the specific financial management needs of small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), startups, and freelancers in Germany and Italy. The founding team included Jessica Holzbach, Lav Odorovic, Luka Ivicevic, Sir Gabriel Holbach, Aleksandar Orlic, and Igor Kuschnir, who brought together experience from the startup and banking sectors. Their shared vision was to create a business banking solution that was more accessible and user-friendly than traditional banking services, which they found to be cumbersome and slow-moving for modern digital businesses.The company provides business bank accounts that can be opened online, streamlining a process that traditionally requires significant paperwork and time. Penta's platform is designed to offer a comprehensive suite of financial tools directly integrated into the bank account. This includes features like multi-user access for team members, expense management tools, and the issuance of debit cards for employees. The business model is centered on a subscription-based service, offering different pricing tiers based on the features and transaction volumes required by the client. This structure allows businesses to scale their banking services in line with their growth.A significant milestone in Penta's journey was its acquisition by the French neobank Qonto in July 2022. This strategic move created a combined entity with a large European customer base of SMEs and freelancers. The acquisition allowed Qonto to become the market leader in Germany and to expand its presence in Italy. For Penta's clients, the integration promised access to a broader range of services and a more extensive European network. The combined platform aims to be the finance solution of choice for SMEs and freelancers across Europe. Keywords: digital banking, business accounts, SME finance, fintech, expense management, subscription model, neobank, financial services, freelance banking, corporate cards
Founders: Jessica Holzbach;Luka Ivicevic;Sir Gabriel Holbach;Matteo Concas;Igor KuschniR;Luka Ivicevic;Sir Gabriel Holbach;Lav Odorovi?;Igor Kuschnir;Aleksandar Orli?

Name: Kadmos
Long description: Kadmos.io is a global salary payments platform that provides a comprehensive solution for secure, end-to-end salary payments. The company primarily serves employers and employees, with a particular focus on migrant workers. Kadmos operates in the financial technology market, offering a unique blend of payroll management and payment services.The company's business model is centered around its automated payment process, which significantly reduces administrative costs for employers. This is achieved through its employer web app, which allows for easy payroll management. Employees, on the other hand, benefit from instant salary payments via the Kadmos mobile app. This app also enables them to manage their money, transfer funds home, and use their Kadmos debit card to make purchases or withdraw cash.Kadmos generates revenue through transaction fees and foreign exchange (FX) rates. The company offers market-leading FX rates, which not only benefits the employees but also makes the platform attractive to employers looking to retain their workforce. Additionally, Kadmos users can make payments to each other for free, further enhancing the platform's appeal.The company recently raised over $29 million in a Series A funding round, indicating strong investor confidence. Notable backers include prestigious venture capital firms such as Blossom, Addition, and Atlantic Labs, as well as angel investors associated with successful fintech companies like Stripe, SumUp, TrueLayer, and Moss.Keywords: Global Salary Payments, Payroll Management, Payment Automation, Migrant Workers, Mobile App, Debit Card, Instant Payments, Foreign Exchange Rates, Venture Capital, Fintech.

Name: Savedo
Long description: Savedo is a financial technology platform that offers high-yield fixed-term deposit accounts to retail investors. The company operates in the European market, partnering with a variety of reputable banks to provide competitive interest rates on savings. Savedo serves individual clients looking to maximize their savings with secure and profitable deposit options. The business model revolves around connecting savers with banks that offer attractive deposit products, earning a commission for each successful deposit facilitated through its platform.
